Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DUCIANA CHANEE FOLASHADE THOMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/5/2023. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) The small estate affidavit must be signed by all the successors with proof of their identify required by Probate Code section 13104. 2) The original small estate affidavit with a certified copy of the death certificate must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached. 3) Paragraph 4 of the small estate affidavit must state the correct maximum value ($184,500). 4) A DE-300 form must be attached to the small estate affidavit. The above issues need to be cleared two weeks prior. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHERINE PATRICIA PRONGOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the accounting period is not set forth on the summary of account or in the petition, as required by Probate Code Section 1061(a), and the bank statements are all for different ending periods. The co-administrators' commingling of estate assets with those of others is explicitly NOT approved. (George is a daughter and Rebecca is a son? In two amended versions, these have remained consistent) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLASSIE WASHINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/22/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 05/09/2023 and also posted as tentative ruling for 06/06/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. 2) Item 3g(d) of the petition must be completed re James Riley as first-named executor. 3) Re item 8: i) The date of death must be provided for the decedent's spouse and two children. ii) The date of death of Moses Riley must be provided. iii) Did Elaine Hubbard leave any issue who survived her? 4) The decedent died more than 6 years ago. Please briefly explain why the petitioner delayed opening this probate. 5) The decedent's last name was spelled "Washinton" on the signature and witness pages of the will. If it was the decedent's aka, it must be added to item 1 of the DE-121 form, and new notice must be mailed and published.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEWEL DENT REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED 8/30/17
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Under Probate Code 850 For Order Determining That Certain Real Property, Located In The Cit
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/20/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/07/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order (hard copy) has been received. 2) Please identify all occupants of the 1142 Fitzgerald property. Each occupant who is not a beneficiary of the trust must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. 3) Please identify all occupants of the 1160 Fitzgerald property. Each occupant who is not a beneficiary of the trust must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. 4) Please provide a copy of a deed or other recorded instrument or a title report showing how the settlor held title to the 1142 Fitzgerald property at the time of her death. 5) Please provide a copy of a deed or other recorded instrument or a title report showing how the settlor held title to the 1160 Fitzgerald property at the time of her death. 6) Please provide the date of death of Mary Harris and identify all her children. 7) Please provide the date of death of Jewell Hall and identify all her children. 8) Please identify all living children of Jewell Freeman. 9) Please identify all deceased children of Jewell Freeman and their living children (grandchildren of Jewell Freeman). 10) Please provide the names and dates of death of the settlor's parents, siblings, and their issue. 11) Please provide the names and addresses all surviving issue of the settlor's parents who would be her heirs. Each heir who is not a beneficiary of the trust must be served in the manner required by Probate Code sections 851(b)(3) and 17203(b), and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. Note: A copy of a verified supplement or other paper identifying the persons and providing other information must be served on each person entitled to notice with a copy of the petition. A proof of service must be filed. See CRC 7.53(b).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALD RAY FUNKHOUSER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The addresses of the interested parties must be set forth, not in care of an attorney. (See description on the Judicial Council form for what information goes in item 8 of a Petition for Letters of Administration.) (2) Unless the interested party has directed in writing that notice be given only to his/her attorney, notice must be given directly to the interested party. [CRC 7.51(a).] (3) How old is the minor? Notice must be given directly to a minor and also to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51 (d).] A second supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address these issues. A courtesy copy of the second supplement and proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/25/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A pleading form of proof of service is never appropriate in Probate. If used, a copy of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate or the Notice of Hearing must be attached to it as proof that the correct notice was served. A supplement does not ordinarily require notice. [Probate Code Section 1205.])
